claim_bullets.bullet_text: The for...of loop works directly with any object that is iterable, meaning it works with any object that has a Symbol.iterator method that returns an object iterator (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.211-215).
The Symbol.iterator method is used to define how objects should return their iterator, avoiding conflicts with existing code (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.211-215).
The for...of loop and the spread operator can be used with any iterable object, enabling more flexible and concise code (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.211-215).
Iterables can be spread into arrays or used as function arguments, making them useful for manipulating collections (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.211-215).

One caveat of spreading iterables is that JavaScript creates an array out of the elements, which can be wasteful for large collections or impossible for infinite ones (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.211-215).

source_record_text: Summary of the 'Basic Operations on Iterables' section from JavaScript Allongé, covering the for...of loop, Symbol.iterator, and spreading iterables.
